jehudah (or judah), the name of five israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory
jerushalaim or jerushalem, the capital city of palestine

JER.27.21.definition: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
properly, like this, i.e., by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
a mass of persons (or figuratively, things), especially reg. organized for war (an army); by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively (speci
gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme god; occasionally applied by way of
he will rule as god; jisral, a symbolical name of jacob; also (typically) of his posterity
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
something prepared, i.e., any apparatus (as an implement, utensil, dress, vessel or weapon)
to jut over or exceed; by implication, to excel; (intransitively) to remain or be left; causatively to leave, cause to abound, preserve
a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)

jehudah (or judah), the name of five israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory
jerushalaim or jerushalem, the capital city of palestine

JER.27.22.definition: babel (i.e., babylon), including babylonia and the babylonian empire
to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
there (transferring to time) then; often thither, or thence
to exist, i.e., be or become, come to pass (always emphatic, and not a mere copula or auxiliary)
as far (or long, or much) as, whether of space (even unto) or time (during, while, until) or degree (equally with)

JER.27.22.gloss: and there shall they be until the day

to visit (with friendly or hostile intent); by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc
properly, self (but generally used to point out more definitely the object of a verb or preposition, even or namely)
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
to ascend, intransitively (be high) or actively (mount); used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative
to turn back (hence, away) transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively (not necessarily with the idea of return to the starting point);
near, with or among; often in general, to
properly, a standing, i.e., a spot; but used widely of a locality (general or specific); also (figuratively) of a condition (of body or mind)
the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that
